context.description: The author reflects on consciousness as a dynamic process that shifts states in sleep and in expanded awareness. The page argues that recurring symbols in everyday life may form a consistent symbolic language across cultures and eras, similar to dream rules. It ends by signaling a transition to the next chapter.
normalization_notes: German prose translated into English control-language summary.
No visible chapter heading or title extracted from the page content.

rows.paragraph_text: Consciousness can assume different states and continually does so, whether to lose complexity when we sleep or to gain breadth when we allow it to. It is in any case a dynamic process whose rules we do not really understand, or do not want to understand.

The author proposes looking for symbolic languages that appear in everyday life without first asking where they come from. Symbolic patterns are described as unusually consistent across cultures and eras, presenting broader meaning and resembling the rules of a dream.

rows.text_content: This will be the subject of the next chapter.
